content.full_text:
August 14, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

FILING REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPONENT(S) OF A LOCAL BALLOT MEASURE

A petition was circulated for qualification of a local ballot measure by a single proponent. Since a ballot measure committee was not formed and there is only one proponent, we reviewed his filing requirements with your analyst Linda Morreau.

Because the circumstances are unique, we would like a written verification of our understanding. A single proponent using his personal funds is not required to file a statement until he receives contributions of $1,000 for qualification of the measure; and at that time he is classified as a committee. If he does receive $1,000 or more, he must file Form 410 (Statement of Organization) as a ballot measure committee and Form 419 (Ballot Measure Committee Campaign Disclosure Statement).

If the proponent continues to use only personal funds, does the total amount of expenditures affect his filing obligations? Also, do the filing requirements change after qualification of the ballot measure?

I would appreciate a written response to clarify our understanding of the proponent's filing obligations as soon as possible.

FRANCES HONG
CITY CLERK

FH:nck
